decisions.summary: Stick with log-tail detection (option b from the issue). Do NOT probe CLIProxy with HTTP (option a) and do NOT add a CLIProxy → dashboard push path (option c).

Broaden the burn-line matcher in applyBurnedTokenOverride to detect BOTH 'refresh token has already been used' (existing, PAN-913 phrasing) AND 'refresh_token_reused' (the literal code-field shape documented in this issue). Never remove the original phrasing — only add.
Adapt the issue's acceptance criterion AC3 from 'mock CLIProxy returning 401' to 'mock the log tail containing refresh_token_reused' so the test exercises the actual code path that exists.
Place backend tests at src/lib/__tests__/codex-auth.test.ts and src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/codex-auth.test.ts, following the project's existing __tests__/ co-located vitest convention.
CodexAuthBanner already renders for status 'burned' (src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CodexAuthBanner.tsx:13). No frontend change is required — fixing the matcher is sufficient to satisfy AC2.

hazards.summary: PAN-913's original case ('refresh token has already been used') MUST continue to be detected. A regression here re-opens PAN-913.
hazards.mitigation: Keep the existing literal in the matcher. Add 'refresh_token_reused' as an additional disjunct. The lib test covers BOTH phrasings as separate cases.

hazards.summary: OpenAI's error-text format could change again, leaving us back where this issue started.
hazards.mitigation: Add a comment immediately above the matcher documenting the two known phrasings and the issues that introduced each (PAN-913 and PAN-1455). Tests pin the exact literals so any future regression is loud.

hazards.summary: The 'burn + later success' suppression at lines 163-173 must still work after the change — a successful /v1/messages 200 line AFTER either burn phrasing should still suppress the burned override.
hazards.mitigation: Lib test must include a case where a 'refresh_token_reused' burn line is followed by a 200 success on /v1/messages and assert status === 'valid' (not 'burned').

hazards.summary: The route test should not depend on the real ~/.panopticon/cliproxy/cliproxy.log of the host running CI/tests.
hazards.mitigation: Override getCliproxyLogPath / getCliproxyAuthDir via env vars or vitest module mocks so the test points at a temp directory with a fixture log and credentials file.

hazards.summary: The 60-minute burn-staleness window (BURN_STALENESS_MS) means a fixture with a stale timestamp will be suppressed. Test fixtures must use a timestamp inside the staleness window.
hazards.mitigation: Generate fixture log lines with timestamps based on Date.now() at test time, OR vi.setSystemTime so a hard-coded timestamp is fresh.

sessionHistory.note: Initial planning session started for PAN-1455
Auto-mode planning complete: confirmed audit findings, chose log-tail matcher widening over HTTP-probe or push-event approaches, decomposed into 3 beads (matcher fix, lib test, route test).
